root_cause.text: A poorly constructed regular expression in `URLValidator` allowed catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS) when processing URLs with long sequences of consecutive slash characters.
root_cause.evidence: The patch changes the vulnerable regex component `\\/+` (one or more slashes) to `\\/{0,1}` (zero or one slash) and adds a test case evaluating a URL with many consecutive slashes (`http://example.com////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////??`) to verify the fix against ReDoS attacks.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: A poorly constructed regular expression in `URLValidator` allowed catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS) when processing URLs with long sequences of consecutive slash characters.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch changes the vulnerable regex component `\\/+` (one or more slashes) to `\\/{0,1}` (zero or one slash) and adds a test case evaluating a URL with many consecutive slashes (`http://example.com////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////??`) to verify the fix against ReDoS attacks.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix URL path validation regex by restricting the number of consecutive slashes allowed.
intent_record.what: Modified the regular expression used for URL validation by changing the path segment separator from '\\/+' to '\\/{0,1}'.
intent_record.how: Restricts the path matching logic to allow only zero or one slash instead of multiple consecutive slashes, correcting validation behavior and potentially preventing regex performance issues.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a test case to verify the URL validator rejects URLs with excessive trailing slashes.
intent_record.what: Added a single assertion to a test file for a URL regex pattern.
intent_record.how: Ensures the system correctly rejects a specific malformed URL with excessive slashes.

grouping_result.intent: Fix URL path validation regex by restricting the number of consecutive slashes allowed.
Add a test case to verify the URL validator rejects URLs with excessive trailing slashes.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly addresses the root cause by modifying the URL validation regex to restrict consecutive slashes, mitigating the catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS) vulnerability.
The group adds a test case specifically targeting the root cause of the vulnerability, which is the validation of URLs with long sequences of consecutive slashes.

candidates.rationale: This line contains nested quantifiers with '(/+...)*', which allows for catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS) when processing URLs with long sequences of consecutive slashes.

vuln_statements.explanation: This regular expression segment contains nested quantifiers '(/+...)*' which causes catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS) when processing URLs with long sequences of consecutive slashes.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: Extends validator to allow set predefined regex used to validate URLs
bic_trace_step.reason: The getUrlRegex() method contains the vulnerable regular expression with the construct '(\\/+([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*' on line 109, which allows catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS) when processing URLs with long sequences of consecutive slash characters.
